rows.title: verification: recruit and schedule named Layer 5 human reviewers
rows.acceptance: Operational/recruiting issue. Acceptance from issue body: (1) identify at least two named reviewers, (2) confirm one is fluent in Japanese AND Vietnamese for L5-2, (3) confirm one is a non-developer for L5-5/L5-6, (4) schedule the real-hardware Zoom session(s), (5) link the resulting acceptance log path verification-evidence/{RC_TAG}/acceptance-log.md. The issue body itself states 'Automation cannot complete this issue.' Unblocks human-only Layer 5 issues #115-#122 and epic #19.
rows.human_clarification_reason: Issue body explicitly states 'Automation cannot complete this issue.' All five acceptance items require human identity, scheduling, and live-meeting outputs that an AI agent cannot satisfy. Needs a human operator to assign reviewers and produce the acceptance-log.

rows.title: MACOS-01 — Spike: macOS capture API, TCC, CoreAudio/BlackHole, and ScreenCaptureKit decision
rows.acceptance: Spike/decision issue. Acceptance from issue body: (1) confidence reaches 1.0 for starting MACOS-02; (2) confidence reaches 1.0 or a follow-up blocker is recorded for MACOS-03; (3) Soundflower and bundled-driver paths are explicitly rejected; (4) TCC remediation path is documented; (5) Opus review confirms the decision record is sufficient. Test outputs: (a) CoreAudio/cpal hello-capture emitting 16 kHz mono i16; (b) 60s BlackHole 2ch capture with sample-continuity measurement; (c) ScreenCaptureKit audio-only prototype or Obj-C/Swift trampoline; (d) permission-denied/TCC behavior from terminal launch; (e) first-sample and steady-state latency on at least one Apple Silicon host. Output is a decision record in issue comments + implementation prerequisites for MACOS-02 and MACOS-03; no production capture code shipped here. Out of scope: shipping production macOS capture code.

rows.title: MACOS-02 — Implement macOS CoreAudio/BlackHole capture MVP
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A1 CoreAudio capture evidence exists; (2) MAC-A2 virtual-device classification evidence exists or is linked; (3) no Windows WASAPI behavior regresses; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: 60s BlackHole capture with sample-continuity >= 0.98 at 16 kHz mono i16; missing capture device surfaces plain-English error; disconnect/reconnect does not crash and is logged with recoverable state; `cargo test` passes on macos-13 and macos-14. Outputs: cfg(target_os = "macos") capture module preserving AudioChunk format; macOS device listing; BlackHole/named-device capture route; evidence under verification-evidence/macos/. Out of scope: ScreenCaptureKit (MACOS-03), bundling drivers. Depends on MACOS-01.

rows.title: MACOS-03 — Implement ScreenCaptureKit system-audio capture and permission preflight
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A4 TCC preflight evidence exists; (2) MAC-A12 ScreenCaptureKit capture evidence exists; (3) i18n/localization explicitly out of scope; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: permission denied exits with code 2 + remediation steps printed; 5-minute ScreenCaptureKit capture continuity >= 0.95; no silent failure when Screen Recording not approved; macOS 14 and macOS 15 behavior recorded. Outputs: capture_source = "system_audio" path; permission preflight check; plain-English remediation message; ScreenCaptureKit capture evidence. Depends on MACOS-01, MACOS-02.

rows.title: MACOS-04 — macOS virtual mic routing via BlackHole/Loopback without bundled drivers
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) MAC-A2 and MAC-A3 evidence exist; (2) no driver binary is bundled or redistributed; (3) Windows VB-CABLE classification still passes; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: classify `BlackHole 2ch`, `Loopback Audio`, `VB-Cable` Mac names; tts_routing = "virtual_mic" resolves to expected route; tts_routing = "both" routes to speakers and virtual mic without duplicate decode; when BlackHole is installed a smoke test writes non-silent PCM to it. Outputs: macOS virtual-device patterns + enumeration/probe; route selection for virtual_mic and both; no-driver-bundling policy in issue evidence. Depends on MACOS-02.

rows.title: CTRL-01 — Real-time input/output volume and gain controls
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOL-A6 evidence exists; (2) runtime controls work without restart; (3) no silent fallback on invalid config; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: gain values clamp at min/max; 0.5 gain produces RMS ≈ 0.5x input RMS within ±2%; hotkey change effective ≤ 50 ms; gain transitions use short ramp/smoothing (no zipper noise); max gain does not clip above target limiter policy. Outputs: input/output gain settings; TUI gauges + hotkeys; watch-channel-based live updates; RMS evidence artifacts. Out of scope: OS-level mixer synchronization (unless separate spike approves).

rows.title: CTRL-02 — Real-time TTS voice catalog and hot-swap
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOICE-A5 evidence exists; (2) voice selection is provider-aware; (3) no concurrent second voice is introduced; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: mocked Google TTS request contains selected voice name; voice swap affects next synthesis call; p95 voice-swap-to-first-audio ≤ 500 ms for next utterance; invalid voice surfaces visible user error; in-flight utterance finishes (not cut) unless policy changes. Outputs: VoiceSelection model; provider voice catalog/listing; TUI voice picker; hot-swap state update path. Depends on CTRL-03. Out of scope: streaming TTS interruption/crossfade.

rows.title: CTRL-03 — Single active voice invariant across single and dual modes
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) VOICE-A8 and VOICE-A9 evidence exist; (2) no existing DM-06 policy regresses; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: property/state tests prove concurrently playing TTS streams ≤ 1; dual mode with primary slot A suppresses slot B audio; dual mode with primary slot B suppresses slot A audio; second playback service/mixer creation returns explicit error or is structurally impossible; voice swap mid-utterance preserves invariant and applies on next utterance. Outputs: single TTS playback owner or mixer; primary-slot policy for dual mode; invariant tests; explicit error/guard for attempted second playback owner. Out of scope: simultaneous bilingual spoken output (forbidden by roadmap).

rows.title: MODEL-01 — Local/remote backend selection contract for STT, MT, and TTS
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) provider contract tests pass; (2) privacy/security review finds no silent cloud fallback; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: all supported local/remote combinations parse and validate; unsupported local pair errors unless explicit cloud fallback configured; no network call occurs without explicit consent/config; when fallback is explicitly enabled a user-visible log line or metric records the fallback for privacy audit. Outputs: backend matrix for STT/MT/TTS; config schema decision; fallback and consent contract; contract tests. Out of scope: implementing every remote provider (contract + minimal tests only).

rows.title: MODEL-02 — macOS local model runtime validation and packaging constraints
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) benchmark/evidence artifact exists; (2) packaging plan excludes large model binaries unless explicitly approved; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: local-stt builds on macos-13/14; local-mt builds on macos-13/14; CPU fallback runs without GPU/Metal; model cache uses `~/Library/Application Support/tui-translator/models` or documented ProjectDirs equivalent; RSS/CPU thresholds recorded against baseline. Outputs: macOS build/runtime validation report; model cache path validation; Metal/CoreML decision or deferral; packaging constraints for models and native libraries. Depends on MODEL-01, CI-01.

rows.title: QA-01 — ISO 25010 + ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 QA plan and traceability matrix
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) QA plan covers functional suitability, reliability, performance efficiency, compatibility, security, maintainability, portability, and interaction capability (ISO/IEC 25010); (2) traceability matrix complete for P0 scope; (3) Opus review confirms standards alignment. Test cases: every P0 requirement maps to ≥ 1 test or evidence item; every WBS implementation issue references ≥ 1 test ID; CI/doc lint fails for missing referenced test IDs once tooling exists; Opus QA review validates standards mapping. Outputs: Master Test Plan deliverable; traceability matrix; Test-ID naming convention; quality threshold table. Out of scope: purchasing copyrighted standards text (use compliant summaries).

rows.title: TEST-01 — Deterministic simulation harness for audio, provider, network, PTY, and virtual mic
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) simulation evidence artifacts generated in a stable schema; (2) L1-L4 can run without a live meeting except explicitly marked hardware tests; (3) Opus review clean. Test cases: audio fixture replayer drives pipeline headlessly; provider mock returns 429/503/slow responses with retry/status verification; PTY resize/cleanup tests pass; virtual mic memory PCM roundtrip validates RMS/drop/latency gates; soak file-source mode emits schema-versioned JSON. Outputs: simulation harness plan; fixture replayer tasks; fake provider/network chaos tasks; virtual mic memory-tier tests; evidence schema. Depends on QA-01. Out of scope: replacing L5 human acceptance.

rows.title: CI-01 — CI matrix expansion for Windows/macOS/features and required gates
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) CI run URL shows required contexts green; (2) branch protection can use the new required checks; (3) no existing Windows gate is weakened; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: tampering Cargo.lock causes --locked jobs to fail; required feature combinations compile on macOS and Windows; beta toolchain failure is allowed-fail only where documented; VMIC hardware-dependent jobs remain skip-safe with explicit evidence. Outputs: Windows + macos-13 + macos-14 CI matrix; MSRV/stable/beta policy; feature matrix; required status check list. Depends on QA-01, TEST-01. Out of scope: signing/notarization, SBOM (SEC-01/REL-01).

rows.title: SEC-01 — Supply-chain/security gates, SBOM, SLSA, and signing policy
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) security gate required before release; (2) no secrets or private paths leak into CI artifacts/logs; (3) SBOM and provenance generated for release assets; (4) tui-security-auditor or Opus security review clean. Test cases: fake secret fixture blocks PR without exposing real credentials; cargo deny failure blocks PR; SBOM validates and is attached to release artifacts; cosign verify succeeds for untampered artifacts and fails after byte tamper; logs/telemetry do not expose Google API keys, sensitive BlackHole device names, or user home paths. Outputs: cargo-deny/audit gate; secret scanning gate; SAST/SARIF plan; CycloneDX/SPDX SBOM; SLSA provenance; cosign/signing policy. Depends on CI-01. Out of scope: procurement of code-signing certificates (REL-01).

rows.title: REL-01 — Cross-platform release packaging, notarization, and GA promotion plan
rows.acceptance: Acceptance from issue body: (1) release checklist includes all CI, security, QA, soak, human acceptance, and Opus review gates; (2) artifacts are signed/notarized where applicable; (3) GA promotion is blocked until all gates pass; (4) Opus review clean. Test cases: lipo -info or equivalent confirms universal/target architecture policy; codesign --verify passes; notarytool submit --wait returns accepted; stapler and spctl verification pass; Gatekeeper first-launch UX validated on a clean VM; rollback drill proves users can return to previous release. Outputs: v2 alpha/beta/GA release plan; macOS packaging workflow; notarization and stapling verification; rollback plan; release-readiness checklist. Depends on CI-01, SEC-01, QA-01, TEST-01, MACOS-02, MACOS-04. Out of scope: Mac App Store distribution, Homebrew tap.
